TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua azazua qinaɫan i Kaɫuyung, matjaeneenem a umaq, aya a tjautsikel.
S.PHON: izua azazua qinaʎan i kaʎujuŋ, macaənəənəm a umaq, aja a cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: izua azazua qinaljan i Kaljuyung, matjaeneenem a umaq, aya a tjaucikel.
S.PHON: izua azazua qinaʎan i kaʎujuŋ, maʦaənəənəm a umaq, aja a ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: There was a village at Kaɫuyung with six households, according to the story.

S.FORM: sa manu a qemaɫup a sema zua i Ɫaitaw.
S.PHON: sa manu a qəmaʎup a səma zua i ʎaitaw.
S.FORM: sa manu a qemaljup a sema zua i Ljaitaw.
S.PHON: sa manu a qəmaʎup a səma zua i ʎaitaw.
S.TRANSL: They went hunting to Ɫaitaw.

S.FORM: ka tsemikel anga maqepu azua izua i Putjazaran i pasalaulauz i Kaɫuyung a namaqepu.
S.PHON: ka ʦəmikəɭ aŋa maqəpu azua izua i pucazaran i pasaɭauɭauz i kaʎujuŋ a namaqəpu.
S.FORM: ka cemikel anga maqepu azua izua i Putjazaran i pasalaulauz i Kaljuyung a namaqepu.
S.PHON: ka ʦəmikəɣ aŋa maqəpu azua izua i puʦazaRan i pasaɣauɣauz i kaʎujuŋ a namaqəpu.
S.TRANSL: When they got back, they gathered at Putjazaran in the lower part of Kaɫuyung.

S.FORM: manu "patsuni aken," aya zua ti sa Tsudjuy, nu vetseluqen a kasiw a papetsevutsevungen.
S.PHON: manu "paʦuni akən," aja zua ti sa ʦuɉuj, nu vəʦəɭuqən a kasiw a papəʦəvuʦəvuŋən.
S.FORM: manu "pacuni aken," aya zua ti sa Cudjuy, nu veceluqen a kasiw a papecevucevungen.
S.PHON: manu "paʦuni akən," aja zua ti sa ʦuɉuj, nu vəʦəɣuqən a kasiw a papəʦəvuʦəvuŋən.
S.TRANSL: “Look at me!” said Tsudjuy, while he broke trees which had been put together.

S.FORM: "aku maya?" aya a salasaladj.
S.PHON: "aku maja" aja a saɭasaɭaɉ.
S.FORM: "aku maya?" aya a salasaladj.
S.PHON: "aku maja" aja a saɣasaɣaɉ.
S.TRANSL: “Why can he do that?” said his companions,

S.FORM: mavilad a sema umaq.
S.PHON: maviɭad a səma umaq.
S.FORM: mavilad a sema umaq.
S.PHON: maviɣad a səma umaq.
S.TRANSL: and ran away to their homes.

S.FORM: ka tjaiviliɫ anga azua ti sa Tsudjuy tjelu anga a qadaw tu qinaɫupan.
S.PHON: ka caiviɭiʎ aŋa azua ti sa ʦuɉuj cəɭu aŋa a qadaw tu qinaʎupan.
S.FORM: ka tjaivililj anga azua ti sa Cudjuy tjelu anga a qadaw tu qinaljupan.
S.PHON: ka ʦaiviɣiʎ aŋa azua ti sa ʦuɉuj ʦəɣu aŋa a qadaw tu qinaʎupan.
S.TRANSL: Afterwards Tsudjuy went hunting for three days.

S.FORM: "aku maya sun?" ayain nua mamazangiɫan.
S.PHON: "aku maja sun" ajain nua mamazaŋiʎan.
S.FORM: "aku maya sun?" ayain nua mamazangiljan.
S.PHON: "aku maja sun" ajain nua mamazaŋiʎan.
S.TRANSL: “Why are you like that?” asked the chief.

S.FORM: manu "aki ken a makuda aya a ventseluq tua kasiw?" aya zazua.
S.PHON: manu "aki kən a makuda aja a vənʦəɭuq tua kasiw" aja zazua.
S.FORM: manu "aki ken a makuda aya a venceluq tua kasiw?" aya zazua.
S.PHON: manu "aki kən a makuda aja a vənʦəɣuq tua kasiw" aja zazua.
S.TRANSL: “Why am I able to break trees like that?”(?) he said.

S.FORM: ɫakua "uri kitjutapaw aken.
S.PHON: ʎakua "uri kicutapaw akən.
S.FORM: ljakua "uri kitjutapaw aken.
S.PHON: ʎakua "uRi kiʦutapaw akən.
S.TRANSL: But “I’ll live in a house apart.

S.FORM: saka nu masan palak aken, maya kidut; matsay mun," ayain azua salasaladj.
S.PHON: saka nu masan paɭak akən, maja kidut; maʦaj mun," ajain azua saɭasaɭaɉ.
S.FORM: saka nu masan palak aken, maya kidut; macay mun," ayain azua salasaladj.
S.PHON: saka nu masan paɣak akən, maja kidut; maʦaj mun," ajain azua saɣasaɣaɉ.
S.TRANSL: When I am possessed, don’t come near; you’ll die,” he said to his companions.

S.FORM: sa vaik azua a seman djalan tu sisa tjatjan.
S.PHON: sa vaik azua a səman ɉaɭan tu sisa cacan.
S.FORM: sa vaik azua a seman djalan tu sisa tjatjan.
S.PHON: sa vaik azua a səman ɉaɣan tu sisa ʦaʦan.
S.TRANSL: He went to make a path leading to a spring.

S.FORM: ka maqatsuvung a seman djalan, seman djalungan pizua i vetsekadan tua djeɫeqi; saka maqatsuvung anga azua sinan tjatjan.
S.PHON: ka maqaʦuvuŋ a səman ɉaɭan, səman ɉaɭuŋan pizua i vəʦəkadan tua ɉəʎəqi; saka maqaʦuvuŋ aŋa azua sinan cacan.
S.FORM: ka maqacuvung a seman djalan, seman djalungan pizua i vecekadan tua djeljeqi; saka maqacuvung anga azua sinan tjatjan.
S.PHON: ka maqaʦuvuŋ a səman ɉaɣan, səman ɉaɣuŋan pizua i vəʦəkadan tua ɉəʎəqi; saka maqaʦuvuŋ aŋa azua sinan ʦaʦan.
S.TRANSL: When he had finished making the path, he made a well in the middle of a rock-face; and so the spring was finished.

S.FORM: saka vaik a kikasiw tu sisan tapaw.
S.PHON: saka vaik a kikasiw tu sisan tapaw.
S.FORM: saka vaik a kikasiw tu sisan tapaw.
S.PHON: saka vaik a kikasiw tu sisan tapaw.
S.TRANSL: Then he went to get wood to make a house.

S.FORM: ɫakua inika masi tjakit, inika masi putjaw; vetseluqen tua lima.
S.PHON: ʎakua inika masi cakit, inika masi pucaw; vəʦəɭuqən tua ɭima.
S.FORM: ljakua inika masi tjakit, inika masi putjaw; veceluqen tua lima.
S.PHON: ʎakua inika masi ʦakit, inika masi puʦaw; vəʦəɣuqən tua ɣima.
S.TRANSL: But he didn’t use a knife or axe; he broke trees with his hands.

S.FORM: saka nu masi kasiw, makataɫ tua sialangan.
S.PHON: saka nu masi kasiw, makataʎ tua siaɭaŋan.
S.FORM: saka nu masi kasiw, makatalj tua sialangan.
S.PHON: saka nu masi kasiw, makataʎ tua siaɣaŋan.
S.TRANSL: When he carried the wood, he carried all the rafters in one journey.

S.FORM: sa kiqatsiɫay tu siupu makapusaɫ, vinatsaq tua sialangan.
S.PHON: sa kiqaʦiʎaj tu siupu makapusaʎ, vinaʦaq tua siaɭaŋan.
S.FORM: sa kiqaciljay tu siupu makapusalj, vinacaq tua sialangan.
S.PHON: sa kiqaʦiʎaj tu siupu makapusaʎ, vinaʦaq tua siaɣaŋan.
S.TRANSL: He collected stones for the walls in two journeys, carrying them with a rafter for a pole.

S.FORM: sa vaik anga a kitsukes; kimaya ḍusa tjataɫ a pasa melava, saka pasa laḍuq kimaya tjelu a tjataɫ.
S.PHON: sa vaik aŋa a kiʦukəs; kimaja ɖusa cataʎ a pasa məɭava, saka pasa ɭaɖuq kimaja cəɭu a cataʎ.
S.FORM: sa vaik anga a kicukes; kimaya drusa tjatalj a pasa melava, saka pasa ladruq kimaya tjelu a tjatalj.
S.PHON: sa vaik aŋa a kiʦukəs; kimaja ɖusa ʦataʎ a pasa məɣava, saka pasa ɣaɖuq kimaja ʦəɣu a ʦataʎ.
S.TRANSL: He went to get pillars; they were about two arm spans wide and three arm spans long.

S.FORM: saka maqatsuvung anga kiniqepu, sa san tapaw anga zazua, aya.
S.PHON: saka maqaʦuvuŋ aŋa kiniqəpu, sa san tapaw aŋa zazua, aja.
S.FORM: saka maqacuvung anga kiniqepu, sa san tapaw anga zazua, aya.
S.PHON: saka maqaʦuvuŋ aŋa kiniqəpu, sa san tapaw aŋa zazua, aja.
S.TRANSL: He finished collecting the materials, and made a house, they say.

S.FORM: saka "aku izua zazua a pupitsul a mamaw tu tsemas?" aya.
S.PHON: saka "aku izua zazua a pupiʦuɭ a mamaw tu ʦəmas" aja.
S.FORM: saka "aku izua zazua a pupicul a mamaw tu cemas?" aya.
S.PHON: saka "aku izua zazua a pupiʦuɣ a mamaw tu ʦəmas" aja.
S.TRANSL: “Why is he as strong as a god?” said his former companions,

S.FORM: saka samaɫi aravats azua salasaladj, aya a tjautsikel.
S.PHON: saka samaʎi aravaʦ azua saɭasaɭaɉ, aja a cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: saka samalji aravac azua salasaladj, aya a tjaucikel.
S.PHON: saka samaʎi aRavaʦ azua saɣasaɣaɉ, aja a ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: very surprised, according to the story.
